sections.title: Before speaking to the Easter Bunny
Talking to Glanton after reading the recipe
Talking to Glanton after finishing the cutscene

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I have some raisins please?

lines.speaker: Glanton
lines.text: I think ye have enough raisins at the moment.

lines.speaker: Glanton
lines.text: Make some space for them and ye can have some more.

lines.speaker: Glanton
lines.text: Of course, we've plenty to spare after that trip.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd like to make some Easter Buns, I don't suppose you have any raisins?

lines.speaker: Glanton
lines.text: And why would you think a man like me would have any raisins?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure, I don't actually know what raisins are to be honest.

lines.speaker: Glanton
lines.text: Well I'll be! Kid, raisins are dried-up grapes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, well do you have any of those?

lines.speaker: Glanton
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet?

lines.speaker: Glanton
lines.text: That's what I said.

lines.speaker: Glanton
lines.text: As it happens I have a sackful of grapes in my possession.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How convenient, dare I ask why?

lines.speaker: Glanton
lines.text: There are great and terrible beasts in this world kid. We'd been hunting them, and believe me when I tell ye, some of 'em had an awful lot of grapes on their person.

lines.speaker: Glanton
lines.text: The slaughter was feverish. We'd cut 'em down and they'd rise up, again and again.

lines.speaker: Glanton
lines.text: Before we knew it, we'd collected a whole sackful o' grapes!

lines.speaker: Glanton
lines.text: Seems to me we could make those into raisins, should that align with your intentions.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What luck! Shall we then?

lines.speaker: Glanton
lines.text: Well alright, it'll be a long journey though.

lines.speaker: Glanton
lines.text: Rinder!

lines.speaker: Rinder
lines.text: What say ye, Glanton?

lines.speaker: Glanton
lines.text: Our friend here needs some raisins, let's head to the desert!

lines.speaker: Rinder
lines.text: Pack yer grapes, then. Let's go.

lines.speaker: Glanton
lines.text: Here we are.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here? This big rock?

lines.speaker: Glanton
lines.text: This big rock is perfect fer makin' raisins, kid. It's real warm.

lines.speaker: Rinder
lines.text: It's as he says kid, real warm.

lines.speaker: Glanton
lines.text: We'll leave the grapes here in good clean heat, give 'em time to think about their transgressions.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't remember this rock being here before.

lines.speaker: Rinder
lines.text: This rock was always here, before man was, even.

lines.speaker: Rinder
lines.text: That you ain't noticed it speaks to yer purview of the world.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm almost certain this is new.

lines.speaker: Rinder
lines.text: Perhaps more things in this world exist without yer knowledge than with it. Did ye consider that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well no, it's just a rock.

lines.speaker: Glanton
lines.text: Alright alright already! Let's get these grapes going.

lines.speaker: Rinder
lines.text: We'd best I suppose. Lay them down and see to their transformation.

lines.speaker: Rinder
lines.text: Hmm, let's see...

lines.speaker: Rinder
lines.text: I judge these grapes to be...

lines.speaker: Rinder
lines.text: ...Plump and juicy! But not fer long!

lines.speaker: Glanton
lines.text: We'll see them desiccated soon enough.

lines.speaker: Rinder
lines.text: It is their destiny. A curse on these grapes!

lines.speaker: Glanton
lines.text: [player name], You've gotta make the grapes feel bad or they won't shrivel up.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh I see, um, curse those grapes! Suffer you foolish berries! Ha!

lines.speaker: Glanton
lines.text: Now yer gettin' it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Insufferable fruits! Rank orbs of low repute!

lines.speaker: Rinder
lines.text: You tell 'em what for!

lines.speaker: Glanton
lines.text: Well look at these! Perfect!

lines.speaker: Rinder
lines.text: They're a fine example of raisins I'll say.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great! let's get back to the Rabbit Hole then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well that was interesting.

lines.speaker: Glanton
lines.text: Yes it was, come back when ye have some space and I'll give ye yer share of raisins.

lines.speaker: Glanton
lines.text: Yes it was. Here's some raisins for yer troubles.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you!

sections.title: Talking to Glanton after giving Eggus Manus a bun

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks for taking me to make raisins.

lines.speaker: Glanton
lines.text: I was plenty happy to educate ye.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I used those raisins to make some Easter Buns, and the Easter Bunny really enjoyed them!

lines.speaker: Glanton
lines.text: Well that's great, kid.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks again for the help.

lines.speaker: Glanton
lines.text: Any time. You owe me one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, I uh, suppose I do.

lines.speaker: Glanton
lines.text: Well met stranger, how do you come to be in this place?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm a friend of the Easter Bunny, I've been looking for him.

lines.speaker: Glanton
lines.text: Is that so? Well that makes you my friend too. Me and Rinder here helped that crazy priest track him down.

lines.speaker: Rinder
lines.text: That we did.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see, that was good of you.

lines.speaker: Glanton
lines.text: I won't lie, it was no selfless act. We've been promised grand rewards.

lines.speaker: Rinder
lines.text: Eggs! Big chocolate eggs!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It always comes down to eggs!

sections.title: Return to Varrock after saving Easter

lines.speaker: Rinder
lines.text: It won't catch on, Glanton.

lines.speaker: Glanton
lines.text: Ah don't be like that Rinder, how will we know till we try?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright gents, what's going on here then?

lines.speaker: Glanton
lines.text: I've had this great idea see.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Does it have anything to do with long walks in the desert again?

lines.speaker: Glanton
lines.text: Well y'see thats the beauty of it.

lines.speaker: Glanton
lines.text: It's a lot easier to melt chocolate than dry out grapes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Chocolate? You've piqued my interest!

lines.speaker: Glanton
lines.text: Exactly! See Rinder, [player name] doesn't even know half the idea yet and wants in!

lines.speaker: Rinder
lines.text: It still seems terribly messy.

lines.speaker: Glanton
lines.text: Okay so, what we do is use our connections with the Easter Bunny to acquire a lot of chocolate.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And give it to me for my outstanding contributions to the preservation of Easter?

lines.speaker: Rinder
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Glanton
lines.text: And we melt it all up, and put it in a fountain.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And then I get to have it all?

lines.speaker: Rinder
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Glanton
lines.text: No then we sell people the right to take a couple of small fruits to dip in the fountain and eat!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That sounds delicious!

lines.speaker: Rinder
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I can get some for free?

lines.speaker: Rinder
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Glanton
lines.text: See the amount people would pay for just a taste of chocolate is so much higher than we'd have to pay to source a lot of the stuff.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I'm not interested if I have to pay.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I saved Easter...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... Again!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I'll go see if the Easter Bunny has some more eggs for me...

lines.speaker: Glanton
lines.text: Be seeing you!
